html:
A Chest is a storage Structure. It requires 3 Boards to build and a Science Machine to prototype. It has 9 storage slots. Items can be stored in a Chest by dragging them onto it or by opening the Chest to place them in specific slots. Chests help keep items better organized than by dropping them on the ground. Animals also cannot eat Food kept in Chests except for Splumonkeys, who can knock items out of them as well as Bearger. Also, Krampus is able to break Chests in order to steal the items inside.

Backpacks, Piggybacks, and Krampus Sacks cannot be stored in Chests, even if the containers are empty. However, they can be used as an alternative storage space if for some reason the player is unable to craft a Chest.

In both Sandbox Mode and Adventure Mode, Chests are occasionally found pre-built in the world. These are natural Set Pieces and they may contain traps set by Maxwell, causing various effects such as:

  • Spontaneous combustion (character and surrounding objects burst into flames).
  • Summer or Winter suddenly occurs.
  • All food in the player's inventory immediately Spoil.

Downloadable Content

In Reign of Giants, during Summer, Chests, as with many structures, can spontaneously combust. A smoking effect will appear, in which time the player must either extinguish the smouldering. Using one's hands causes damage. If this is not done in time, the object will combust and burn. The alternative way to solve this is by using a Scaled Chest.

As with Backpacks, an Insulated Pack cannot be placed in a Chest.

In Shipwrecked, Thatch Packs, Sea Sacks, Booty Bags, and Warly's Chef Pouch cannot be placed inside a Chest.

In Hamlet, Pogs can open Chests and empty them by taking all of the items stored. If they find any food in them, they will eat it. Like other Backpacks, the Vortex Cloak cannot be placed inside a Chest.

Trivia

  • In the old research system, the research cost of a Chest was 100 research points.

Bugs

  • If a Chest is burned, the option to open it may appear, and if the player tries to open it the game will crash.

Gallery

  • An open Chest.
  • "Store" appears when a storable item is dragged over a Chest.
  • A Set Piece trap with a Chest that, when opened, has a chance to cause Winter to immediately arrive.
  • A Set Piece with a Chest that has a chance to catch fire when opened.
  • A burnt Chest from the Reign of Giants DLC.
  • Unused Chest emoji from official Klei Discord server.png
  • Unused Engry Chest emoji from official Klei Discord server.png
  • Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.

“It is my tickle trunk!”
–Wilson
“It's a trunk for my junk.”
–Willow
“I put stuff here!”
–Wolfgang
“For which to contain my lucre.”
–Wendy
“EXTERNAL STORAGE UNIT”
–WX-78
“A storage chest.”
–Wickerbottom
“Wood is so handy! Look at all the things you can make!”
–Woodie
“A place where to store loot.”
–Maxwell
“Has a lock, but it does not require a key. Curious.”
–Wagstaff
“A place tö störe my helm and spear while I rest.”
–Wigfrid
“We could keep our toys in it!”
–Webber
“Treasure!”
–Walani
“Treasure!”
–Warly
“Aye, th' meaning o' life inna box.”
–Woodlegs
“A BOX FOR'TH WILBA THINGS”
–Wilba
“Stuff!”
–Wormwood
“More pockets!”
–Wheeler
“Handmade, so you know it's not up to snuff.”
–Winona
“A storage place for bits and bobs.”
–Wortox
“Oooooh, box for things!”
–Wurt
“It isn't a toy chest, it's a very grownup SUPPLY chest.”
–Walter
“Less cluttering up my pockets means less cluttering up my mind.”
–Wanda

A '''Chest''' is a storage [[Structure]]. It requires 3 [[Boards]] to build and a [[Science Machine]] to prototype. It has 9 storage slots. [[Items]] can be stored in a Chest by dragging them onto it or by opening the Chest to place them in specific slots. Chests help keep items better organized than by dropping them on the ground. [[Animals]] also cannot eat [[Food]] kept in Chests except for [[Splumonkey]]s, who can knock items out of them as well as Bearger. Also, [[Krampus]] is able to break Chests in order to steal the items inside.

[[Backpack]]s, [[Piggyback]]s, and [[Krampus Sack]]s cannot be stored in Chests, even if the containers are empty. However, they can be used as an alternative storage space if for some reason the player is unable to craft a Chest.

In both [[Sandbox Mode]] and [[Adventure Mode]], Chests are occasionally found pre-built in the [[world]]. These are natural [[Set Pieces]] and they may contain traps set by [[Maxwell (NPC)|Maxwell]], causing various effects such as:
*Spontaneous [[Fire|combustion]] ([[character]] and surrounding objects burst into flames).
*[[Summer]] or [[Winter]] suddenly occurs.
*All food in the player's inventory immediately [[Rot|Spoil]].
__NOTOC__
== {{Pic32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content ==
In ''[[Reign of Giants]]'', during [[Summer]], Chests, as with many structures, can spontaneously combust. A smoking effect will appear, in which time the player must either extinguish the smouldering. Using one's hands causes damage. If this is not done in time, the object will combust and burn. The alternative way to solve this is by using a [[Scaled Chest]].

As with Backpacks, an [[Insulated Pack]] cannot be placed in a Chest.

In [[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|''Shipwrecked'']], [[Thatch Pack]]s, [[Sea Sack]]s, [[Booty Bag]]s, and [[Warly|Warly's]] [[Chef Pouch]] cannot be placed inside a Chest.

In ''[[Hamlet]]'', [[Pog]]s can open Chests and empty them by taking all of the items stored. If they find any [[food]] in them, they will eat it. Like other [[:Category:Backpacks|Backpacks]], the [[Vortex Cloak]] cannot be placed inside a Chest.

== {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* In the old research system, the [[Research points list|research cost]] of a Chest was 100 research points.

== {{Pic32|Mosquito}} Bugs ==
* If a Chest is burned, the option to open it may appear, and if the player tries to open it the game will crash.
